breaks.caption: Sunridge · Whistler, BC · Matt Anthony Photography
Sunridge · Whistler, BC · Matt Anthony Photography

sections.lead: I crawled 30 pages on lrdstudio.ca. The site is well-built with good meta data, a 36-URL sitemap, and fast response times. But one issue is undermining all that photography.

findings.title: Good Technical Foundation
findings.detail: Valid SSL, 91ms response, 36-URL sitemap, proper canonical tags, Open Graph. Solid Squarespace build.

findings.title: Strong Meta Title
findings.detail: "LRD Studio | Interior Design - Whistler, Squamish" — clear, branded, includes key locations.

findings.title: 20 Project Pages
findings.detail: Extensive portfolio with 34.8 images per page on average. Strong visual coverage.

findings.title: 464-Word Homepage
findings.detail: Good content depth. Communicates the studio’s philosophy and approach.

findings.title: IDIBC Registered + Board Member
findings.detail: Lauren Ritz is a registered NCIDQ professional, IDIBC member, and sits on the IDIBC board. Professional credentials are in place.

findings.title: 699 of 761 Images Missing Alt Text (91.8%)
findings.detail: This is the single biggest issue on the site. Nearly every image is invisible to Google. For an interior design studio where visual discovery is everything, this means Google Image search (a major source of design-intent traffic) can’t index any of your work.

findings.title: No Structured Data (Schema.org)
findings.detail: Zero JSON-LD markup. Google doesn’t know LRD is an interior design studio, where you’re based, or what services you offer. Adding LocalBusiness + InteriorDesigner schema would improve local search presence.

findings.title: Meta Description Slightly Long (163 chars)
findings.detail: Google truncates at ~155 characters. Trim by 8 characters so the full description displays in search results.

findings.title: No Blog or Journal
findings.detail: No content strategy driving organic traffic. Articles targeting “interior design Whistler,” “Sea-to-Sky interior designer,” or material trend pieces would capture high-intent search traffic.

insight.title: SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: The site’s technical foundation is solid. The critical fix is alt text on 699 images, a one-time project that permanently makes your entire portfolio visible to Google Image search. Combined with schema markup, these two fixes would significantly change how the studio appears in search results.

sections.lead: When a homeowner in Whistler or Squamish searches “interior designer near me,” can they find LRD Studio?

findings.title: No Google Business Profile Detected
findings.detail: LRD Studio doesn’t appear in Google Maps or the Local Pack. For a studio with a physical address in Squamish serving the Sea-to-Sky corridor, this is the single highest-ROI fix available.

findings.title: No LocalBusiness Schema
findings.detail: No structured data connecting the website to a local business identity.

findings.title: Listed on Houzz
findings.detail: Active Houzz profile. Important for interior designers.

findings.title: Not Found on HomeStars
findings.detail: Consider creating a profile for citation building.

findings.title: Not Found on IDIBC Directory
findings.detail: The Interior Designers Institute of BC directory is an authoritative backlink. Verify membership listing is active.

insight.title: Local SEO Bottom Line
insight.content_html: Google Business Profile is the #1 priority. Free, 30 minutes. Add the Squamish address, service areas (Whistler, Pemberton, Squamish, Vancouver), project photos, and request 5-10 Google reviews from past clients. For a design studio competing with Ashley de Boer (who has a Squamish Chamber listing and multiple award nominations), this is the foundation of local visibility.

sections.lead: Here’s how LRD Studio’s online presence stacks up against interior designers competing for the same clients in the Sea-to-Sky corridor.

cells: NKBA 30 Under 30, Georgie + HAVAN finalist 2025

insight.title: The Competitive Gap
insight.content_html: Ashley de Boer is gaining ground fast with multiple 2025 nominations. But Lauren has the Western Living finalist credential and IDIBC board seat. The gap isn’t credentials, it’s ongoing visibility: video content, social presence, and continued award submissions to maintain momentum. No Sea-to-Sky designer has professional video content yet.

sections.lead: LRD Studio’s photography from our work together is strong. Here’s where to build from.

findings.title: 20 Project Pages
findings.detail: Strong portfolio depth with professional photography from our sessions. Averaging 34.8 images per page.

findings.title: Professional Photography
findings.detail: Images from our Sunridge and other sessions. Design-intent photography that shows material choices, light, and spatial composition.

findings.title: Clear Brand Voice
findings.detail: The site communicates LRD’s design philosophy well. West coast modern influences, material sensitivity, and client-centered approach.

findings.title: Western Living DOTY Finalist + Build Magazine Feature
findings.detail: Lauren Ritz was a 2022 Western Living Designer of the Year finalist. Also featured in Build Magazine (Vancouver & Whistler). Real credentials that competitors respect.

findings.title: 91.8% of Images Invisible to Google
findings.detail: 699 images with no alt text. For an interior design studio, Google Image search is one of the most valuable discovery channels. Homeowners search “modern kitchen Whistler” or “log home interior renovation” and the results are images. LRD’s portfolio can’t appear in any of them.

findings.title: No Video Content
findings.detail: Zero project walkthrough videos, zero material reveal reels, zero design process content. For interior design, atmospheric video showing how light moves through a space is the highest-impact content type.

findings.title: No Blog or Design Journal
findings.detail: No content targeting “interior design Whistler” or “Sea-to-Sky interior designer.” Design trend pieces, material stories, and project behind-the-scenes content would drive organic traffic.

insight.title: Content Bottom Line
insight.content_html: LRD’s photography portfolio is genuinely strong from our work together. The gaps are discoverability (alt text, SEO), media format (no video), and credentials (no awards submitted). Fixing the alt text alone would make 20 projects visible to Google Image search overnight.

sections.lead: Three moves that would have the biggest impact on LRD Studio’s visibility.

steps.title: Fix 699 Image Alt Tags + GBP
steps.description: Alt text across the portfolio is a one-time project that permanently makes every project visible to Google Image search. GBP setup adds Google Maps presence. Together, these unlock the two biggest discovery channels.

steps.title: Video + Atmospheric Content
steps.description: Interior design sells through atmosphere. A 60-second walkthrough showing how light enters a Sunridge room tells a story that photos alone can’t. No Sea-to-Sky designer has this content. First mover wins.

steps.title: Continue the Awards Momentum
steps.description: The Western Living finalist credential opened the door. Keep submitting to IDIBC Awards of Excellence, Georgie, and HAVAN. Each new project photographed to spec is another potential entry. Don’t let the pipeline go cold.

insight.title: This Is Where a Creative Partner Comes In
insight.content_html:
Every gap in this audit is something I handle. Professional photography with keyword-rich alt text on every image, atmospheric video, social content, and the awards pipeline. One relationship, one monthly investment, and every project LRD designs starts working for the studio’s visibility.

See the full Creative Partner proposal for pricing, options, and a pilot roadmap.
